_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of information systems and organizational requirements (relevant to confidentiality, integrity, availability, authentication, non-repudiation).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data subject rights management, including rights request intake, fulfillment workflows, response timelines, and documentation requirements.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.579), SP-K-014 (SP, cosine: 0.576).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.405). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, requirements]. DCWF-only terms [authentication, availability, confidentiality, information, integrity, non] vs WIDAI-only terms [data, documentation, fulfillment, intake, management, request] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.879 (P=0.877, R=0.881). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). "Knowledge of information systems and organizational requirements (relevant to co" and "Knowledge of data subject rights management, including rights request intake, fu" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.592 > STS 0.405 (gap +0.186). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.8%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, requirements]. DCWF-only: [authentication, availability, confidentiality, information, integrity, non]. WIDAI-only: [data, documentation, fulfillment, intake, management, request].

signals.detail: STS 0.405 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.186 with low STS 0.405. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
